entries.description: Reconstruction note: Session 1's work landed in commit 1a973be ('ai_router 0.2.3: state-first lifecycle protocol') but activity-log.json was not authored during the session. This file is reconstructed from the commit message + router-metrics.jsonl to satisfy close-out gates. The work itself is on disk and was cross-provider verified (verification rounds logged below).

Added ai_router/start_session.py: CLI entry point that infers next session via compute_effective_completed_sessions, refuses to advance past an open session (exit 3), refuses to re-open closed sessions, refuses to skip ahead. Idempotent re-entry for in-flight sessions. Wraps register_session_start.

Added compute_effective_completed_sessions() to ai_router/session_state.py. Read order: completedSessions[] (authoritative) → distinct closeout_succeeded events (Full-tier fallback) → legacy currentSession-1 heuristic (last resort, stderr warning). Single source of truth for 'how many sessions closed.'

Updated _flip_state_to_closed in session_state.py to append currentSession to completedSessions[] (sorted, unique) on every close. Final-session detection now uses len(completedSessions) == totalSessions post-append, with change-log.md presence as belt-and-suspenders. register_session_start preserves completedSessions[] across snapshot rewrite (backfills from events for legacy sets).
Extended close_session --repair --apply Case 1 to backfill completedSessions[] directly from the events ledger using the new helper. Case 2 trigger narrowed for idempotency under the new semantics.

Added ai_router/tests/test_start_session.py (idempotency, refuses-N+1-while-N-open, refuses-skip-ahead, refuses-reopen-closed, currentSession inference, work_started emission/dedupe). Extended test_close_session_session4.py with completedSessions[] append assertions on every close. Extended test_repair_detects_mixed_mode_drift to assert backfill. 699 tests pass.
Bumped ai_router to 0.2.3 (pyproject.toml + __init__.py).

Cross-provider verification round 1 (gpt-5-4, $0.21): VERIFIED. Then verification round 2 (gemini-pro, $0.02): VERIFIED. Both providers concurred the state-first lifecycle protocol matches the spec invariant; completedSessions[] writer semantics correct; --repair --apply backfill path sound.

Pre-Session-2 cleanup: Session 1 had committed in 1a973be but was never formally closed via close_session — the events ledger had only the work_started event, no closeout_succeeded. Authored disposition.json + activity-log.json (reconstructed from commit message + router-metrics.jsonl, see session-001/reconstruction-note), committed as 66ad890, then ran `python -m ai_router.close_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/022-active-lifecycle-management --json`. All 5 gates passed; closeout_requested + closeout_succeeded events emitted; completedSessions[] became [1] via the new writer Session 1 just shipped. Then ran `python -m ai_router.start_session ...` which flipped currentSession to 2 and emitted work_started. The state-first lifecycle protocol Set 022 delivered is now running on its own session set.

src/utils/fileSystem.ts: removed activity-log unique-sessionNumber count derivation and the currentSession-1 fallback. New count order: completedSessions.length (primary) → countDistinctCloseoutSessions(eventsPath) (Full-tier fallback; new helper generalizes hasCloseoutEventForSession to a count) → totalSessions when state==='done'. Activity-log read retained for totalSessions metadata and per-entry dateTime (lastTouched signal); count branch dropped. Also added `eventsPath` resolution + surfaced `completedSessions: number[] | null` through LiveSession so the tree-view can compute the in-flight predicate without re-reading the snapshot.

src/extension.ts: extended the bindWatchers RelativePattern to include `session-events.jsonl` and `CANCELLED.md`. Required so a fresh-set Not Started → In Progress flip on session 1 doesn't wait for the 30s poll loop (start_session writes ledger + state file; the prior pattern only watched the state file), and so a cancel/restore command's CANCELLED.md write fires an immediate refresh.

src/providers/SessionSetsProvider.ts: added isCurrentSessionInFlight(set) implementing the spec invariant (currentSession not in completedSessions[]); guards against legacy snapshots by requiring completedSessions to be a non-null array. progressText now appends ` Done` on done rows and ` · session N in flight` when the predicate fires. Both functions exported for unit-test reuse.

src/test/suite/fileSystem.test.ts: added 5 regression tests — events-ledger fallback with dedupe, no-array-no-ledger renders 0/N (not currentSession-1), activity-log no longer inflates count, liveSession.completedSessions surfaced, and a new countDistinctCloseoutSessions suite (missing file, distinct counting, dedupe, malformed-line tolerance). src/test/suite/sessionSetsProvider.test.ts: new file with 13 tests covering isCurrentSessionInFlight (legacy snapshot, fresh set, between-sessions, mid-set in-flight) and progressText (Done annotation, in-flight annotations at both endpoints, base fraction for legacy / not-started). forceClosedBadge.test.ts fakeLive helper updated to include the new completedSessions field. TypeScript compiles cleanly (tsc --noEmit exit 0). The .vscode-test electron harness is currently broken on this machine (1.119+ Code.exe rejects --no-sandbox); ran a standalone smoke harness with a vscode stub instead: 14/14 pure-logic assertions pass, plus a live readSessionSets call against set 022 renders `1/3 · session 2 in flight` as expected.
Bumped extension to v0.13.12 across package.json, package-lock.json, CHANGELOG.md (added a v0.13.12 section detailing all four behavior changes), and CLAUDE.md (current version line).

`npx vsce package` → DONE Packaged: dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.13.12.vsix (19 files, 398.91 KB). Build is shippable. Live readSessionSets call against the real session-set tree confirmed 22 sets bucket correctly (done=18, in-progress=3, not-started=0, cancelled=1) and set 022 renders the expected `1/3 · session 2 in flight` annotation.

Cross-provider verification round 1 (gpt-5-4, $0.15): ISSUES_FOUND with 2 Major findings. (1) Correctness — fallback count path uses merged totalSessions variable that prefers activity-log over state file; if activity-log declares a stale totalSessions, the done fallback would compute the wrong count. Real edge case for Lightweight-tier hand-edits. (2) Completeness — verifier claimed package.json / package-lock.json / CLAUDE.md weren't updated. Determined to be a context-gap false positive: those files WERE updated in commit dcc8636 but my verification prompt only listed CHANGELOG.md in the files block. Fixed in subsequent step.

Fixed round-1 Major finding 1: `src/utils/fileSystem.ts` readSessionSets() now always lets `sd.totalSessions` (state file) override the activity-log-sourced value when present, rather than only when the merged variable was still null. Added regression test 'totalSessions: state-file value beats activity-log value' in fileSystem.test.ts. Tsc --noEmit clean; live readSessionSets smoke-test against set 022 still renders the expected `1/3 · session 2 in flight`.

Cross-provider verification round 2 (gpt-5-4, $0.20): VERIFIED, issues: []. Round-2 prompt was expanded to include package.json + CLAUDE.md + a package-lock.json version-line excerpt so the round-1 finding-2 context gap couldn't recur; an explicit note in the verification scope explained finding-1 was fixed (with regression test) and finding-2 was a context-gap false positive now resolved. Verifier concurred. Total Session 2 verification spend: $0.344.

Started Session 3 via `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/022-active-lifecycle-management --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort medium` before any other work. State file flipped currentSession 2 -> 3, lifecycleState stayed work_in_progress, completedSessions preserved as [1, 2], orchestrator metadata refreshed; work_started event appended. The set is running on its own state-first protocol — same recursive self-demo Session 2 set up.

docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md: Step 1 ('Identify the Active Session Set and Register Session Start') gained an explicit 'State first, work second (Set 022)' subsection with the canonical three-line invariant, a Full-tier CLI invocation (with both bash and Python subprocess pseudo-code), and a Lightweight-tier hand-write template carrying every required field. Step 8 gained a 'Symmetric close protocol (Set 022)' subsection with a non-final-vs-final field-change table and notes on Full-tier writer behavior vs Lightweight hand-maintenance; final-session detection uses len(completedSessions) == totalSessions with change-log.md as belt-and-suspenders. The 'do not skip close_session' warning from commit 7166754 retained and cross-referenced to the new start protocol.

docs/session-state-schema.md: added a 'State invariant (Set 022 — canonical)' section directly under the title carrying the load-bearing three-line invariant (in-flight / between-sessions / done). Added completedSessions to the Required-fields JSON schema example. Promoted completedSessions[] from 'optional but planned' to 'always written on Full / always maintained on Lightweight,' with explicit writer-responsibility callouts for each tier. Updated the parser cheat-sheet to (a) add the events-ledger fallback step and (b) drop the currentSession-1 reader-side fallback (matches extension v0.13.12 reader change). Added an 'in flight' predicate computation block. Updated Full-tier mid-set worked example to include completedSessions and an explanatory note. Migration section now declares the array required on both tiers as of Set 022.

ai_router/docs/close-out.md: added Section 0 'Session-boundary writes (start and close)' covering why two writers exist, field-by-field protocol tables for both start and close, idempotency notes, and tier symmetry. Extended Section 5 drift-case-1 description to call out Set 022's --repair --apply behavior: the apply path also backfills completedSessions[] from compute_effective_completed_sessions (which sees synthesized closeout events), so a drifted set's snapshot and ledger come into agreement on the same boundary write. Updated the table of contents to include Section 0.

dabbler-platform: walked 38 session sets. 0 carry completedSessions[]. 8 Full-tier sets (have session-events.jsonl) will heal cleanly via the events-ledger fallback on their next start_session / close_session call: admin-user-creation-flow, admin-user-creation-flow-uat-remediation, admin-users-cross-links, composable-crud-helpers, packaging-and-template-readiness, transactional-system-columns, uat-dsl-verify-input-value, unified-master-details-composite. 30 Lightweight-tier sets continue under hand-maintenance; their hand writes do not invoke the helper, so no stderr warning fires unless one ever transitions to Full-tier CLI writes.

dabbler-access-harvester: walked 33 session sets. 0 carry completedSessions[]. 5 Full-tier sets will heal cleanly via events-ledger fallback: access-object-extractor-spike, form-report-code-and-grouping, generalization-validation-on-non-northwind, structured-form-report-extractor, table-extractor-coverage. 28 Lightweight-tier sets continue under hand-maintenance. Edge cases noted: integration-testing-and-acceptance (cancelled mid-flight) and vba-symbol-resolution-and-enrichment (retired/superseded) are terminal states and won't see another boundary write — no action needed.

dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb: walked 6 session sets. 4 are fully compliant with numeric completedSessions[] arrays (001-forms-detail-uat [1,2,3,4], 002-forms-browse-uat [1,2,3,4], 005-cleanup-sweep [1,2], 006-finalize-and-publish [1,2,3]). 2 sets carry string-based session IDs in completedSessions[] (003-reports-client-svc-uat, 004-reports-provider-uat) — these violate the schema doc's integer-array contract but are *present and non-empty* so the backfill helper won't fire. Both are terminal-state Lightweight sets in a Lightweight-tier-candidate repo; surfacing as a follow-up for the consumer repo if/when those sets need to interoperate with numeric ledger semantics. No action from this session set.

Cross-provider verification across three rounds (all via gpt-5-4 since orchestrator is claude-opus-4-7 and the verifier complexity score / max_tier consistently selected GPT). Round 1 ($0.096): ISSUES_FOUND with 2 Major findings, both in the homehealthcare-inventory narrative — a counting error (5 numeric + 2 strings ≠ stated 6 sets) and a verdict that labeled all 6 as compliant despite calling out 2 non-conformant. Round 2 ($0.163): ISSUES_FOUND with 1 Minor finding — the Lightweight-tier bullet narrative said hand writes would emit a stderr warning, but the helper isn't called on hand writes (only on Full-tier CLI writes). Reworded both Lightweight bullets and the verdict summary to distinguish the two cases. Round 3 ($0.159): VERIFIED, issues: []. Total Session 3 verification spend: $0.4175. All findings were in the verification narrative, not in the doc edits themselves — the workflow, schema, and close-out doc changes verified clean on every round.
